meta.title: Live-Working Risk Assessment — Energized LV Switchboard SB-2
meta.subtitle: De-energize-first evaluation + EAWR reg-14 live-working justification + energized-work permit (NFPA 70E Annex J)
meta.author: HSE Utilities & Power
meta.classification: Internal — competent-person review required; live work must be sanctioned by a competent authority
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. A prior contact/arc-flash incident is cited at role level as context; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: Thermographic survey on the energized LV switchboard SB-2 (400 V), Substation 2. The task is performed within the limited approach boundary of exposed live conductors. Assessment is task- and conductor-specific (not 'work near the live parts'). De-energization was evaluated FIRST; arc-rated PPE is the documented last line, not the headline control.

The default is to establish an electrically safe work condition (NFPA 70E Article 120 / EAWR dead-working). For an in-service thermographic survey, de-energizing the board removes the thermal load the survey exists to measure, so dead working defeats the purpose of the task — the de-energization evaluation is recorded BEFORE the justification, and live work is treated as the exception. A task that could be done dead would be recommended for an ESWC and would NOT be assessed live.

Live work is permitted ONLY where ALL three statutory tests hold: (a) it is UNREASONABLE to work dead — de-energizing the board defeats the in-service thermal survey it exists to perform; (b) it is REASONABLE to work live — a non-contact survey within a controlled approach boundary by a qualified person; (c) SUITABLE PRECAUTIONS are taken — approach-boundary control, no-contact technique, arc-rated PPE selected against the cross-referenced incident energy, and an energized-work permit. A justification of convenience / cost / 'production cannot stop / it's quicker' is REJECTED — economic convenience alone never justifies live work.

sections.caption: Limited/restricted approach boundaries (shock) defined; the arc-flash incident energy + PPE category are cross-referenced from arc-flash-assessment, never re-derived in this skill

rows.title: Task proposed live but de-energization not initially evaluated
rows.description: The work was initially scoped as 'just do it live, it's quicker'. A bare convenience reason is NOT a valid EAWR reg 14 / 1910.333 justification — the de-energization evaluation and the three-part statutory test were applied before any live work was accepted; the convenience reason was REJECTED and replaced with the recorded reg-14 justification (in-service survey is unreasonable to perform dead).
rows.evidence: Intake Q2/Q3; prior contact incident IR-role-A (de-identified)

rows.title: Approach-boundary control needed during the survey
rows.description: Without a defined restricted approach boundary and a no-contact technique, the survey would risk shock contact with exposed 400 V conductors.

sections.text: Hierarchy-ranked precautions (de-energize first → arc-rated PPE last)

rows.control: De-energize + establish an ESWC (Article 120) — the default; applied wherever the task can be done dead
Non-contact survey technique + fixed/insulated barriers between the worker and the live conductors
Approach-boundary control + qualified-person & accompaniment requirement + energized-work permit
Arc-rated PPE selected against the cross-referenced incident energy — the documented LAST line, never the headline control

sections.text: A live-working plan whose ONLY control is 'wear the arc-flash suit', with no recorded de-energization evaluation, is NOT accepted — it is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy. Arc-rated PPE is the residual-hazard last line; the de-energization decision and the statutory justification come first.
Energized electrical work permit (NFPA 70E Annex J content)

rows: Description of the energized work + apparatus
Thermographic survey, energized LV switchboard SB-2 (400 V)
EAWR reg 14 three-part test met (recorded verbatim); convenience reason rejected
Limited/restricted approach boundaries (130.4); non-contact technique; insulated barriers
Cross-referenced from arc-flash-assessment (#1); arc-rated PPE per the computed category
Qualified Electrical Worker + Authorised Person accompaniment
Competent authority sign-off (role) — REQUIRED before live work proceeds
sections.caption: Energized-work permit (NFPA 70E Annex J) — issued only after the de-energization evaluation + the three-part statutory justification

rows.action: Cross-reference the arc-flash incident energy + PPE category from arc-flash-assessment before the survey (no re-derivation)
Confirm the limited/restricted approach boundaries for 400 V (NFPA 70E 130.4) and brief the work party
Issue + authorise the energized-work permit (Annex J content) with the recorded justification

items: Arc-flash incident energy at the working position is cross-referenced from arc-flash-assessment (#1); a missing arc-flash study is a [GAP], never an invented cal/cm² value.
Where the cross-referenced incident energy exceeds 40 cal/cm², the live work is flagged PROHIBITED.
India site: state electricity-rule line-clearance return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; no national form number minted).
NFPA 70E (2024) — 110.5/130.2 (energized-work justification + permit), 130.4 (shock approach boundaries), Annex J (energized electrical work permit), Article 120 (the de-energize-first ESWC default)
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.333(a)(2) (de-energize-first / additional-hazard-or-infeasible justification) + 1910.269 (T&D minimum approach distances)
UK EAWR 1989 reg 14 (the three-part live-working test) + HSG85 + HSR25; India CEA / state electricity rules via hse-india
Review trigger: per live-work operation / on equipment change / on procedure change — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person must review and a competent authority must sanction any live work before use.
